title: Smoky Zesty Braised Festive British Lamb Tagine
description: A hearty, comforting lamb tagine that combines the rich flavors of smoked paprika and caraway seeds with the brightness of lemon and the warmth of British spices.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large tagine pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, caraway seeds, British allspice, salt, black pepper, red chili flakes, and cayenne pepper. Stir to combine and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lemon zest, lemon juice, British ale, and glassy-jelly. Stir to combine and bring to a simmer.
Transfer the tagine to the oven and braise for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the tagine from the oven and stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the lamb and sauce hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Serve with couscous or crusty bread, if desired.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving, to allow the flavors to meld together.
Reheat the tagine over low heat, if necessary, before serving.
Reheat the sauce separately, if desired, before serving.
Reheat the glassy-jelly separately, if desired, before serving.
Garnish with additional glassy-jelly, if desired, before serving.
Garnish with additional chopped parsley, if desired, before serving.
Serve with additional lemon wedges, if desired, before serving.
Garnish with additional red pepper flakes, if desired, before serving.
Garnish with additional preserved lemon, if desired, before serving.
Serve with a dollop of remaining glassy-jelly, if desired, before serving.
